free_text: Nuclear reactions of V with 300-GeV protons and Co
with 11.5-, 200-, and 300-GeV protons
The 200- and 300-GeV irradiations
were performed in an external proton beam in the
neutrino hall at the National Accelerator Laboratory
(now Fermi Lab - FNAL).
Two irradiations of Co were at 11.5-
GeV were made using the circulating beam of the ANL
zero-gradient synchrotron
After irradiation samples were counted without
any chemical separation with ca1ibrated Ge(Li)
spectrometers
Reaction products were produced by spallation
reaction
It was assumed, that cross section is constant at all
measured energies i.e. at 10, 28 and 300 GeV
Compiled by S.H.
The uncertainties quoted represent
estimates of the precision based on the fit of the
decay curves with half-lives literature. The
data of tab.1 are based on three independent runs.
Data taken from tab. 1 of the reference
51V sample with dimensions 6x6 cm2. The target foils
were irradiated together with aluminum monitor foils,
and each was sandwiched between guard foils to avoid
cross contamination. Total thickness of V stack was
150mg/cm2 and 30mg/cm2
